_meta.note: Entry keyed to SA1_Revision_Notes.json chapter numbering (Ch2 = Long-term products). All sp1_refs and section_map subsection ids verified against SP1_Revision_Notes.json.

sp1_refs.note: IP policy structure, incapacity definitions, benefit amount and timing design, risk and rating factors, product variations — SA1 adds India-specific IP features, IRDAI regulatory constraints, and application-level insurer perspective.

CI conditions covered, definitional boundaries (TPD, terminal illness, children's benefit), tiered benefits — SA1 extends with Indian CI market landscape, regulatory treatment, and customer-need framing.

LTCI pre-funded vs immediate needs distinction, ADL/cognitive impairment triggers, benefit types, premium structures, unit-linked and pension-based variants — SA1 adds India-specific long-term care context and regulatory overlay.

section_map.alignment_note: Introductory orientation to IP — history, definition and aims — provides the definitional baseline for SA1's comparative overview of long-term products.
CI introduction and key characteristics are part of the cross-product overview that SA1 Ch2 opens with.
LTCI definition and the informal/formal care distinction are foundational context for the SA1 long-term product overview.
History, definition/aims, and product cycle map directly to SA1 sub 1.1 (Benefits & Features) — establishes what IP is and how it works.

Full benefit design section covers all three SA1 subsections: 2.1–2.4 (conditions, amount, timing, incapacity definition) feed SA1 sub 1.1; 2.5 (risk/rating factors) feeds SA1 sub 1.2 (Insurer's Perspective); 2.6 (product variations) feeds SA1 sub 1.3 (Meeting Customer Needs).
CI introduction and characteristics map to SA1 sub 2.1 (Benefits & Features) — what CI is, lump-sum vs IP distinction, key product characteristics.

Core and additional conditions, terminal illness, TPD, children's benefit map to both SA1 sub 2.1 (Benefits & Features) and sub 2.2 (Insurer's Perspective) — condition definitions and boundary-setting are key insurer design decisions.
Tiered benefit design and rationale map to SA1 sub 2.3 (Meeting Customer Needs) — tiering is the primary mechanism for matching CI benefits to varying severity of customer need.
Definition of LTC and informal/formal care distinction feed SA1 sub 3.1 (Benefits & Features) — establishes the care need that LTCI products address.
Pre-funded vs immediate needs distinction feeds SA1 sub 3.1 (Benefits & Features) — fundamental product taxonomy that underpins all subsequent LTCI design choices.
ADL/cognitive impairment claim triggers and benefit types map to SA1 sub 3.1; premium structures and product structure map to SA1 sub 3.2 (Insurer's Perspective).
Immediate needs product features and structure complement pre-funded coverage in SA1 sub 3.1 (Benefits & Features) — together they complete the LTCI product spectrum.

Unit-linked and pension-based LTCI solutions map to SA1 sub 3.3 (Meeting Customer Needs) — variants are the SP1-level articulation of how LTCI design adapts to different customer savings/protection preferences.
